title: rubus longipes
intro: Rubus longipes, also known as the thimbleberry or western thimbleberry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family. The plant is rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and tannins, which contribute to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Rubus longipes include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting digestive health, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Rubus longipes can be prepared as teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal use, and is also used in jams and preserves for its sweet-tasting fruit.
